reviews.review_msg: Sitara is a VERY intelligent and a passionate instructor, that much was clear. I found I enjoyed listening to her but not learning from her. Both the assigned readings and language that she used were challenging and undigestible. truly, it was frustrating. She had told the class that it was the only way to present the material, but I beg to differ

very knowledgeable, prepare to have your perspective changed. If you are passionate about studying Feminism, Racism, Colonialism ect. this is the class for you. WITH THAT SAID this is by no means an easy course. Attendance is taken, readings are mandatory, & assignments are written in class to avoid AI. Can be a tough grader, but she is wonderful.

The grading criteria for this class was all over the place. She is a very tough grader and will give similar grades to very different levels of work. She does want everyone to understand the material and gives an engaging lecture, but I just was not a fan of this class and the way it was graded.

Mariam is an amazing prof, she cares about her students and their learning. The assignments and in class essays weren't hard but you do need to do the readings or you will fall behind in discussions. Overall great course, and the lectures are very informative and engaging. the only down side is that its a 3 hour lecture so not everyone likes that
